title: cirrus: use fastvm for builds
body:
Builds now take over 10 mins, given golang compilation is parallelized by default we can give more cores to speed it up.

<!--

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

```release-note

```

comments.body: This seems to safe around 3mins, not a lot but at least something. I will look to see what parts of the build jobs are slow to see if there are some ways to make it faster because AFAICT most time does not seem to be spend compiling so I think there is some room in our docs/validation process.
Cockpit tests failed for commit 5d2390e07c1bfde20bc48295974897e66f371e06. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
Cockpit tests failed for commit d9e0107f9610b01eb890196e64e8276be63182c6. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

This should now safe around more than 4min of total CI time now.

Another PR on main with a Finished time of 10:22 from cirrus:
 ![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3288e588-9e68-4407-b72b-9e1e4dc288d1)

This PR with a Finished time of 05:51 from cirrus:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d830ac13-2b36-4186-a4c5-af8487b538ce)
@edsantiago PTAL (low prio) I think this is good to review, of course this will fail CI until https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/24227 is merged then I will rebase and mark as ready.

no need to squash. Thank you!
